questions.text: Consider a car is travelling for one hour. In which of the following cases is the average velocity zero?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The car travels 20 km due east.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: The car travels 60 km due east, then turns around and travels 40 km due west.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: The car travels 70 km due east.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

options.text: The car travels 30 km due west, then turns around and travels 30 km due east.
options.explanation: We know that average velocity = total displacement/total time.The car travels 30 km due west, then turns around and travels 30 km due east.Since displacement is a vector quantity and needs direction.Let's assume that positive displacement is towards the west and negative displacement is towards the east.Therefore:Average Velocity = +30 + (-30)/1= 0/1= 0 km/hr.

questions.explanation: We know that average velocity = total displacement/total time.The car travels 30 km due west, then turns around and travels 30 km due east.Since displacement is a vector quantity and needs direction.Let's assume that positive displacement is towards the west and negative displacement is towards the east.Therefore:Average Velocity = +30 + (-30)/1= 0/1= 0 km/hr.

questions.text: A vector such as the velocity of a body undergoing uniform translational motion, which can be displaced parallel to itself and applied to any point known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A vector whose magnitude is one is called a unit vector.

A free vector is one whose action is not confined or associated with a unique line in space. For a body in uniform translational motion, the velocity of any point in the body may be taken as a vector and this vector will equally well describe the velocity of all points in the body.
A directionless vector whose magnitude is zero is called a null vector.
A position vector is defined as a vector that symbolizes either the position or the location of any given point concerning any arbitrary reference point.
A resultant vector is defined as a single vector that produces the same effect as is produced by a number of vectors collectively.

questions.explanation: Option B is the correct answer because a free vector is one whose action is not confined or associated with a unique line in space. For a body in uniform translational motion, the velocity of any point in the body may be taken as a vector and this vector will equally well describe the velocity of all points in the body. Hence, the velocity of such a body may be represented by a free vector.

questions.text: The vectors A and B are such that |A + B| = |A – B|, then the angle between the two vectors is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Cosine of 90° is zero, which does not apply in this scenario.
Incorrect, as the angle between the vectors is not 60° based on the given information.
Correct! By using the properties of vector magnitudes and the given equation, the angle between the vectors A and B is indeed 90°.
Not the correct answer. The angle between the vectors A and B is not 180° as per the equation provided.

questions.explanation: Consider the angle between A and B as ∅. The resultant of A + B vector is R = √(A²+B²+2AB cos∅) and the resultant of A - B vector is R = √(A²+B²-2AB cos∅). Given |A+B| = |A-B|, we can equate the expressions and solve for cos∅ to find ∅ = 90°. This is the correct angle between the vectors based on the provided equation.The other options are incorrect because they do not align with the properties of vector magnitudes and the equation given. Option A and D provide angles that are not supported by the equation, and Option B is based on a misinterpretation of the cosine function in this context.

questions.text: A particle moves from position r= 3i+2j-6k to r= 14i+13j+9k under the action of a Force F= 8i+2j+6k. Find the work done by the force:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

According to the calculation given below, this is the correct answer. Total Work Done= work done in x + work done in y + work done in z Since, Work done= Force x distance moved in the direction of forceDistance moved from r1 to r2 is= r2 – r1 = (14i+13j+9k) - (3i+2j-6k) = (11i+11j+15k)Force= 8i+2j+6kHence, Work done (x)= force (x) x Distance moved in x-axis = 8 x 11 = 88 Work done (y)= force(y) x Distance moved in y-axis = 2 x 11 = 22 Work done (z)= force (y) x Distance moved in z-axis = 6 x 15 = 90Total work done= 88 + 22 + 90=200

questions.explanation: Work done = force×displacement Given data:F=8i+2j+6k N Now displacement, d=(14i+13j+9k) — (3i+2j-6k)d=(14–3)i+(13–2)j+(9-[-6])k d=11i+11j+15k Now, W=F×d W= (8i+2j+6k) × (11i+11j+15k) W=(11×8)+(2×11)+(6×15)W=88+22+90 W=200J

questions.text: In three dimensional Cartesian Coordinate system (x,y,z), you have two vectors A and B. Vector A has components Ax= 4, Ay=3, and Az=2, while vector B has components Bx=1, By=5, and Bz=2. What is dot (scalar product) of vectors A and B?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: To find the dot product (scalar product) of vectors A and B, you multiply the corresponding components and add them together:A · B = Ax_Bx + Ay_By + Az_Bz= (4)_(1) + (3)_(5) + (2)_(2)= 4 + 15 + 4= 23So, the correct answer is b. 23.The dot product is a scalar value that represents the amount of "similarity" between the two vectors. In this case, the result is positive, indicating that the vectors have some degree of alignment.
The dot product is positive, not negative.
The vectors are not orthogonal (perpendicular), so the dot product is not zero.
This is not the correct result of the calculation.

questions.explanation: To find the dot product (scalar product) of vectors A and B, you multiply the corresponding components and add them together:A · B = Ax_Bx + Ay_By + Az_Bz= (4)_(1) + (3)_(5) + (2)_(2)= 4 + 15 + 4= 23So, the correct answer is b. 23.The dot product is a scalar value that represents the amount of "similarity" between the two vectors. In this case, the result is positive, indicating that the vectors have some degree of alignment.

questions.text: If the unit vectors i, j, k are perpendicular to each other therefore;i.i = j.j = k.k = _.I.j = j.k = k.i = _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the dot product of a unit vector with itself is always 1, not 0.
This is the correct answer because the dot product of a unit vector with itself (e.g., i.i) is 1. The dot product of two different perpendicular unit vectors (e.g., i.j) is 0.
This option is incorrect because the dot product of a unit vector with itself is not 0; it is 1.
This option is incorrect because the dot product of two different perpendicular unit vectors is 0, not 1.

questions.explanation: The dot product of two vectors A and B is given by A.B = |A||B| cosθ. If the angle θ between the vectors is 0°, cosθ = 1, so a vector dotted with itself (i.i, j.j, k.k) is 1, due to the unit magnitude of the vectors. If θ is 90°, cosθ = 0, so the dot product of two perpendicular vectors (i.j, j.k, k.i) is 0. Therefore, the correct answer is 1 for i.i = j.j = k.k, and 0 for i.j = j.k = k.i. The other options are incorrect as they do not accurately reflect these properties of the dot product for unit vectors.

questions.text: The vector product of two vectors A and B is _ vectors A and B.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Not equal to the product of magnitudes of
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The vector product, or cross product, results in a vector, not a scalar, and its magnitude is given by |A||B|sin(θ), not just the product of magnitudes.
This option is incorrect. The cross product results in a vector that is perpendicular to, not parallel to, the plane containing the original vectors.
Correct! The cross product of two vectors A and B results in a vector that is perpendicular to the plane containing A and B, as determined by the right-hand rule.

options.text: Less in magnitude than the product of magnitudes of
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The magnitude of the cross product is based on |A||B|sin(θ), and it can be less than, equal to, or greater than the simple product of magnitudes, depending on the angle between the vectors.

questions.explanation: The vector product, or cross product, of two vectors A and B results in a vector perpendicular to the plane containing A and B. This is determined using the right-hand rule, which helps visualize the direction of the resulting vector. The magnitude of the cross product is |A × B| = |A||B|sin(θ), where θ is the angle between A and B. Option C correctly identifies the perpendicular nature of the resulting vector. Option A is incorrect because it misrepresents the nature of the cross product as a scalar. Option B is incorrect because it misinterprets the orientation as parallel rather than perpendicular. Option D is incorrect because it inaccurately describes the magnitude relationship without considering the sine component.

options.text: Two vectors are multiplied to give a scalar
options.explanation: This option is correct. The scalar product, also known as the dot product, is obtained by multiplying two vectors and yielding a scalar quantity. This is achieved by multiplying the magnitudes of the vectors and the cosine of the angle between them.

options.text: Sum of two scalars is taken
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the scalar product involves multiplication of two vectors to produce a scalar result, not the addition of two scalar quantities.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C:Two vectors are multiplied to give a scalar. The scalar product, also known as the dot product, is obtained by multiplying two vectors and yielding a scalar quantity. This is achieved by multiplying the magnitudes of the vectors and the cosine of the angle between them. Options A, B, and D are incorrect because they do not accurately describe the process of obtaining a scalar product.

questions.text: Two forces each of 10N magnitude act on a body. If the forces are inclined at 30° and 60° with x- axis, then the x-component of their resultant is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The x-component of the resultant force is not equal to the magnitude of the individual forces. Consider the components of each force separately.
This value is close but not correct. Make sure to use the correct trigonometric functions to find the x-components of the forces.
Correct! By breaking down the forces into their x-components and adding them, we find that the x-component of the resultant force is approximately 13.66N.
This value is too large. Double-check your calculations and ensure you are using the correct trigonometric functions for finding x-components.

questions.explanation: To find the x-component of the resultant force when two forces of 10N magnitude are inclined at 30° and 60° with the x-axis, we need to find the x-components of each force individually and then sum them.The x-component of the first force is given by: F1x = 10N * cos(30°). The x-component of the second force is given by: F2x = 10N * cos(60°). Adding these components gives us the resultant x-component: Resultant x = F1x + F2x = (10N * cos(30°)) + (10N * cos(60°)) ≈ 13.66N. Therefore, the x-component of the resultant force is approximately 13.66N.

questions.text: The resultant of two forces has magnitude of 20N. If one of the forces is of magnitude 20√3 N and makes an angle of 30o with the resultant then, the other forces must be of magnitude:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Please refer to the main explanation text.

To calculate the magnitude of the other force, we can use trigonometry and vector addition. The given force is 20√3 N at an angle of 30 degrees. Let's break down the force into x and y components:The x-component of the force = 20√3 * cos(30°) = 20NThe y-component of the force = 20√3 * sin(30°) = 10NSince the resultant force is 20N, and one force is already 20N, the other force should balance out the y-component, which is 10N. Therefore, the magnitude of the other force must also be 20N to cancel out the y-component and give a resultant of 20N.

questions.text: If A + B= 7i^ +7k^ and A-B= -i^ + k^, then the magnitude of A will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option a is incorrect.
This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option b is incorrect.

Given: A + B = 7i^ + 7k^ A - B = -i^ + k^ To find the magnitude of A, we need to eliminate B from these equations. We can do this by adding the two equations together: (A + B) + (A - B) = (7i^ + 7k^) + (-i^ + k^) Simplifying the left side and right side separately: 2A = 6i^ + 8k^ Dividing both sides by 2: A = 3i^ + 4k^ Now we have the vector A. To find its magnitude, we can use the formula: |A| = √(Ax^2 + Ay^2 + Az^2) In this case, Ax = 3, Ay = 0, and Az = 4. Substituting these values into the formula: |A| = √(3^2 + 0^2 + 4^2) = √(9 + 0 + 16) = √25 = 5 Therefore, the magnitude of A is 5. Hence, option C is the correct answer.
This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option d is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Given:A + B = 7i^ + 7k^A - B = -i^ + k^To find the magnitude of A, we need to eliminate B from these equations. We can do this by adding the two equations together:(A + B) + (A - B) = (7i^ + 7k^) + (-i^ + k^)Simplifying the left side and right side separately:2A = 6i^ + 8k^Dividing both sides by 2:A = 3i^ + 4k^Now we have the vector A. To find its magnitude, we can use the formula:|A| = √(Ax^2 + Ay^2 + Az^2)Since i^,j^, and,k^ are the unit vectors for x,y, and z coordinate planes respectively.In this case, Ax = 3, Ay = 0, and Az = 4. Substituting these values into the formula:|A| = √(3^2 + 0^2 + 4^2)= √(9 + 0 + 16)= √25= 5Therefore, the magnitude of A is 5. Hence, option C is the correct answer.

questions.text: Which of the following group of quantities represent the vectors
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Acceleration and Force are vector quantities, but mass is a scalar quantity.
Displacement and Velocity are vector quantity but mass is a scalar quantity.
Acceleration and force are vector quantities, but electric flux is a scalar quantity.
Velocity, Momentum, and Electric field are all vector quantities; hence, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: The physical quantities for which both magnitude and direction are defined distinctly are known as vector quantities. For example, a boy is riding a bike with a velocity of 30 km/hr in a north-east direction. Then, as we see for defining the velocity, we need two things, i.e. the magnitude of the velocity and its direction. Therefore, it represents a vector quantity.

questions.text: If two vectors lie in xy-plane, their cross product lies
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If two vectors lie in the xy plane their cross product can not lie in the same plane. Three planes exit i.e. x, y and z which also exist at right angle to each other. If two vectors lie in any of the two planes then their cross product will lie in the third plane and also perpendicular to the planes in which the vector lies.

If two vectors lie in the xy plane their cross product can not lie adjacent to the plane. Three planes exit i.e. x, y and z which also exist at right angle to each other. If two vectors lie in any of the two planes then their cross product will lie in the third plane and also perpendicular to the planes in which the vector lies.
Point index finger in the x-plane, middle finger in the y-plane, your thumb will point in the direction of the cross product of the two vectors in xy-plane.

If two vectors lie in the xy plane their cross product can not lie parallel to the plane. Three planes exit i.e. x, y and z which also exist at right angle to each other. If two vectors lie in any of the two planes then their cross product will lie in the third plane and also perpendicular to the planes in which the vector lies.

questions.explanation: The Explanation for this question is as follows:Point index finger in the x-plane, middle finger in the y-plane, your thumb will point in the direction of the cross product of the two vectors in xy-plane.

questions.text: The magnitude of a given vector with end points (4,–4, 0) and (–2,–2, 0) must be :
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Re-evaluate your calculations using the correct formula for magnitude.
This is incorrect. Ensure you apply the correct formula for finding the magnitude of a vector.
This is incorrect. Double-check the components of the vector and the formula used.
Correct! The magnitude is found using the formula: √((x2-x1)² + (y2-y1)² + (z2-z1)²).

questions.explanation: The magnitude of a vector is calculated using the formula: √((x2-x1)² + (y2-y1)² + (z2-z1)²). For the given endpoints (4, -4, 0) and (-2, -2, 0), the differences in coordinates are: Δx = -2 - 4 = -6, Δy = -2 + 4 = 2, Δz = 0 - 0 = 0. Substituting these into the formula gives: √((-6)² + (2)² + (0)²) = √(36 + 4 + 0) = √40 = 2√10. Therefore, the correct answer is 2√10. Options 6, 5√2, and 4 are incorrect as they do not match the result of the calculation using the formula.

questions.text: The magnitude of product vector C i.e. A × B = C , is equal to the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the magnitude of A × B is not related to the sum of the adjacent sides.
The magnitude of A × B is equal to the area of the parallelogram formed with A and B as two adjacent sides. Hence, this option is correct.
This option is incorrect because the magnitude of A × B is not related to the product of all four sides.
This option is incorrect because the magnitude of A × B is not related to the perimeter of the parallelogram.

questions.explanation: The magnitude of the cross product of vectors A and B is equal to the area of the parallelogram formed by A and B as two adjacent sides. This relationship is a fundamental property of vector cross products in geometry. The correct option, 'Area of the parallelogram,' aligns with this geometric interpretation. The other options are incorrect as they do not accurately describe the geometric significance of the cross product magnitude.

questions.text: Let we have a vector F, then its vertical component is written as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A: This option correctly represents the formula for the vertical component of a vector. It involves the magnitude of the vector and the sine of the angle.
Option B: This option represents the formula for the horizontal component of a vector, not the vertical component. It uses the cosine of the angle instead of the sine.
Option C: This option does not correctly represent the formula for the vertical component of a vector. It uses the tangent of the angle, which is not applicable in this context.
Option D: This option is not the correct formula for the vertical component of a vector. It simply multiplies the magnitude of the vector by the angle without involving any trigonometric function.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A:Fy = |F|sinθThis formula accurately represents the vertical component of a vector by considering the magnitude of the vector and the sine of the angle. The vertical component is crucial in understanding the projection of the vector onto the y-axis. Options B, C, and D provide incorrect formulas or misinterpretations of the vertical component calculation.

questions.text: The resultant of two forces each of magnitude F is 2F, then the angle between them will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. When the resultant of two forces is 2F and each force is F, the angle between them is not 120°.
This is incorrect. When the resultant of two forces is 2F and each force is F, the angle between them is not 30°.
This is incorrect. When the resultant of two forces is 2F and each force is F, the angle between them is not 60°.
Correct! When the resultant of two forces is equal to the sum of the two forces, the angle between them is 0°.

questions.explanation: When the resultant of two forces each of magnitude F is 2F, it means that the two forces are acting in the same line. In this case, the angle between them is 0°. This is because the forces are collinear and add up to produce a resultant along the same line.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the vector quantity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Acceleration is a vector quantity because it involves both magnitude and direction. It is the rate of change of velocity, which is also a vector quantity. When an object accelerates, both the speed and direction change.
Power is a scalar quantity as it only has magnitude. It represents the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred.
Density is a scalar quantity because it only has magnitude. It refers to the mass of a substance per unit volume.
Volume is a scalar quantity as it only has magnitude. It represents the amount of space an object occupies.

questions.explanation: Acceleration is the correct answer as it is a vector quantity due to its magnitude and direction components. Power, density, and volume are scalar quantities because they only have magnitude and do not involve direction.

questions.text: Angular momentum is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Angular momentum is not a scalar. Scalars have only magnitude and no direction. Angular momentum, on the other hand, has both magnitude and direction, so it cannot be a scalar.

Angular momentum is not a polar vector. Polar vectors have a magnitude and direction, but they are defined by their components in a coordinate system. Angular momentum does not fit this definition, so it is not a polar vector.

Angular momentum is an axial vector. Axial vectors have magnitude, direction, and a sense of rotation. They are used to represent quantities that involve rotation, such as angular momentum. So, option (c) is the correct one!

Linear momentum is a different concept from angular momentum. Linear momentum refers to the motion of an object in a straight line, while angular momentum refers to the rotation of an object around an axis. So, option (d) is not the correct one.

questions.explanation: In summary, the correct option is (c). Angular momentum is an axial vector. It has both magnitude and direction, representing rotational motion. The other options are incorrect because angular momentum does not fit the definitions of a scalar, polar vector, or linear momentum, as it involves both magnitude, direction, and rotational characteristics.

questions.text: In contrast of a scalar a vector must have a
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Vector quantities require both magnitude and direction to be defined distinctly. For example, velocity is a vector quantity because it has both a numerical value (magnitude) and a specific direction.
Weight is a scalar quantity as it only has magnitude (numerical value) and no specific direction associated with it.
This option is vague and does not relate to the specific requirement of direction for a vector quantity. Therefore, it is incorrect.
This option is incorrect because a vector must have a direction in addition to magnitude, distinguishing it from scalar quantities.

questions.explanation: A vector must have both magnitude and direction defined distinctly, which is why the correct answer is 'Direction'. Scalars, on the other hand, only have magnitude without any directional component. The other options ('Weight', 'Quantity', 'None of the above') do not address the essential characteristic of direction needed for a quantity to be classified as a vector.

questions.text: In graphical addition of vectors
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The position of vectors is unimportant
options.explanation: Incorrect. While the position of vectors can vary when using the graphical method, altering their order can lead to incorrect results.

options.text: The order of vectors is not to be altered
options.explanation: Correct! The order of vectors must be maintained when adding them graphically using the head-to-tail method to obtain the correct resultant.

options.text: The direction of resultant is unknown
options.explanation: Incorrect. The direction of the resultant vector is determined by the order and direction of the vectors being added graphically.

options.text: The position of vectors is important
options.explanation: Incorrect. The position can vary, but it is the order of vectors that should not be altered in graphical addition.

questions.explanation: The graphical method of adding vectors involves the head-to-tail approach, where each vector is added sequentially, maintaining their respective directions and magnitudes. The order is crucial because reversing or altering the sequence will affect the resultant vector, potentially leading to incorrect conclusions. Therefore, the correct answer is that the order of vectors is not to be altered. The other options are incorrect because they either misrepresent the importance of order or incorrectly state that the direction or position determines the resultant vector in the graphical method.

questions.text: The length of the arrow represents the _ of a vector
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Length cannot determine the direction of a vector.
The length of the arrow represents the magnitude of a vector, making this the correct choice.
While length does indicate magnitude, it does not determine direction.
This option is not directly related to the length of an arrow representing a vector.

questions.explanation: When dealing with vectors, the length of the arrow corresponds to the magnitude of the vector. Magnitude refers to the size or quantity represented by the vector. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Magnitude'. The other options are incorrect because length alone does not determine direction, the combination of direction and magnitude, or the resultant of the vector.

questions.text: Which of the following is correct?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because scalars only have magnitude, not direction.

questions.text: The position vectors of P and Q are 5i+4j+ak and -i +2j - 2k respectively. If the distance between them is 7, then the value of a will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: These are the correct values for 'a' that satisfy the distance condition between the vectors.
This option assumes incorrect modifications of the vectors or miscalculated the distance formula.
This set of values does not satisfy the given distance condition when calculated.
This option does not correctly solve the equation derived from the distance formula.

questions.explanation: To find the value of 'a', use the distance formula for points in 3D space: Distance = √((x2 - x1)2 + (y2 - y1)2 + (z2 - z1)2). Here, P = (5, 4, a) and Q = (-1, 2, -2). The distance is given as 7. Set up the equation: √((5 + 1)2 + (4 - 2)2 + (a + 2)2) = 7. Solving this gives (6)2 + (2)2 + (a + 2)2 = 49. Simplifying, 36 + 4 + (a + 2)2 = 49. Therefore, (a + 2)2 = 9, which gives a + 2 = ±3. Hence, a = 1 or a = -5. The correct answer is –5, 1. The other options result from incorrect calculations or assumptions.

questions.text: If the x-component of the resultant is negative and its y-component is positive, the result is true for:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: An angle of (180°- θ) with x-axis

options.text: An angle of (180°- θ) with y-axis
options.explanation: This option does not correspond to the scenario described in the question where the x-component is negative and the y-component is positive.
An angle of 90° does not align with the situation of a negative x-component and a positive y-component in the second quadrant.
An angle of 180° does not fit the conditions specified in the question regarding the signs of the x and y components.

questions.explanation: When the x-component of the resultant vector is negative and the y-component is positive, the vector is positioned in the second quadrant of the Cartesian coordinate system. In this quadrant, the x-coordinate is negative, and the y-coordinate is positive, leading to an angle of (180°- θ) with the x-axis.The other options are incorrect because they do not align with the specific conditions provided in the question, such as the signs of the x and y components and the quadrant in which the vector lies.

questions.text: When the multiplication of two vectors result into a vector quantity, then the product is called _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The cross product is also known as the vector product, resulting in a vector that is perpendicular to the two vectors being multiplied.
The dot product yields a scalar quantity, not a vector, when two vectors are multiplied together.
Calculating the magnitude of vectors involves using the Pythagorean theorem, it is not the result of multiplying two vectors together.
Since the product of two vectors results in a vector quantity in this case, this option does not apply.

questions.explanation: The cross product, also known as the vector product, results in a vector that is perpendicular to the two vectors being multiplied. It is denoted by a × b and can be calculated using the Right-hand Rule. The dot product of two vectors is a scalar quantity, not a vector, while the magnitude of vectors is determined using the Pythagorean theorem. Therefore, the correct answer is the Cross product.

questions.text: A horse is pulling a cart exerting a force of 100 N at an angle of 30 to one side of motion of the cart. Work done by the horse as it moved 20m is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The calculation incorrectly uses a smaller component of the force. Make sure to calculate the correct horizontal force component.
This option is correct. The work done is calculated by multiplying the horizontal component of the force by the distance moved.
This option is incorrect. It seems to use an incorrect angle component. Ensure you are using the cosine of the angle to find the horizontal force component.
This option is incorrect. It appears to ignore the angle of the force. Remember to consider the angle when calculating the effective force.

questions.explanation: The work done by a force is calculated as the product of the force component in the direction of motion and the distance moved. Here, the horse exerts a force of 100 N at an angle of 30° to the direction of motion. The horizontal component of the force is 100 N × cos(30°) = 86.6 N. The work done is then 86.6 N × 20 m = 1732 J. Option B is correct because it uses the correct horizontal force component to calculate work done. Options A and C use incorrect force components, and Option D ignores the angle entirely, leading to incorrect results.

questions.text: Average velocity is defined as _.

options.explanation: This option is correct Average velocity is calculated by dividing your displacement by the total time.Average velocity =displacement/time
This Option is incorrect because Distance/time is the formula of speed.
This option is not a formula of any physical quantities.
This option is not a formula of any physical quantities.

questions.explanation: Correct option is A.Average velocity is calculated by dividing your displacement (a vector pointing from your initial position to your final position) by the total time; average speed is calculated by dividing the total distance you traveled by the total time.Average velocity =displacement/time

questions.text: Decrease in velocity per unit time is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity per unit time. It can be positive (speeding up) or negative (slowing down). For example, when a car speeds up, its velocity increases and its acceleration is positive. When a car slows down, its velocity decreases and its acceleration is negative. Deceleration, on the other hand, is a type of acceleration that occurs when an object slows down. It is also known as negative acceleration. When an object decelerates, its velocity decreases and its acceleration is negative. So, a decrease in velocity per unit time is not acceleration because it is the opposite of acceleration. It is deceleration.
A decrease in velocity per unit time is not positive acceleration. It is the opposite of positive acceleration and is called deceleration.

Deceleration is the rate at which an object slows down. It is measured as the decrease in velocity per unit time, and is given by the formula: deceleration = (final velocity - initial velocity) / timewhere "final velocity" is the velocity of the object at the end of the time interval, "initial velocity" is the velocity of the object at the beginning of the time interval, and "time" is the duration of the interval.If the final velocity is less than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is negative, indicating that the object is slowing down. If the final velocity is greater than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is positive, indicating that the object is speeding up.

A decrease in velocity per unit time is not uniform acceleration. Uniform acceleration refers to a constant rate of change in velocity over time, either positive or negative. Deceleration, on the other hand, is not uniform acceleration because the rate at which the velocity decreases is not constant over time. The rate of deceleration may change depending on the object's initial velocity, its mass, and the forces acting on it.

questions.explanation: Deceleration is the rate at which an object slows down. It is measured as the decrease in velocity per unit time, and is given by the formula: deceleration = (final velocity - initial velocity) / timewhere "final velocity" is the velocity of the object at the end of the time interval, "initial velocity" is the velocity of the object at the beginning of the time interval, and "time" is the duration of the interval.If the final velocity is less than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is negative, indicating that the object is slowing down. If the final velocity is greater than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is positive, indicating that the object is speeding up.

questions.text: For a linear relationship between displacement and time, we get a d-t graph as?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is correct. For a linear relationship between displacement and time, the d-t graph will be a straight line that passes through the origin (0, 0). This indicates that when time (t) is zero, the displacement (d) is also zero, and as time increases, the displacement increases linearly.

options.text: Straight line parallel to t- axis
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. If the d-t graph is a straight line parallel to the t-axis, it would indicate that the displacement remains constant over time. This represents a constant or zero velocity, not a linear relationship between displacement and time.

This option is incorrect. A quadratic relationship between displacement and time would result in a d-t graph that is a parabolic curve, not a straight line. A quadratic relationship indicates that displacement changes with the square of time, not linearly.

This option is incorrect. A cubic relationship between displacement and time would result in a d-t graph that is a cubic curve, not a straight line. A cubic relationship indicates that displacement changes with the cube of time, not linearly.

questions.explanation: This option is correct. For a linear relationship between displacement and time, the d-t graph will be a straight line that passes through the origin (0, 0). This indicates that when time (t) is zero, the displacement (d) is also zero, and as time increases, the displacement increases linearly.

questions.text: S.I. unit of acceleration is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it is unit of length.
This option is incorrect because it is unit of area.
This option is incorrect because it is unit of velocity
This is the correct representation of unit of acceleration.

questions.explanation: Unit of Acceleration that is universally accepted for the measurement of acceleration is ms-2. Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity with respect to time. Acceleration can also be expressed in some other units like Gal, Standard Gravity (g), and Planck Acceleration (ap).
